text.title: Ad Creative, Dynamic Creative, and Naming Conventions
text.summary: Image and video creative upload flow, call-to-action types, image specifications per placement, dynamic creative setup, and naming convention for campaigns/ad sets/ads.

list.item: Image ad: 1) upload image to /adimages (returns hash), 2) create creative with image_hash, 3) create ad referencing creative_id
Video ad: 1) upload video to /advideos (async), 2) poll status until "ready", 3) create creative with video_id, 4) create ad

rule.statement: Check video upload status before creating the creative. Creating a creative with a video in non-ready state returns a validation error.
rule.rationale: Video processing is asynchronous and can take minutes. Polling is required; there is no webhook for upload completion in the basic API.

rule.statement: Name campaigns, ad sets, and ads with a consistent pattern that encodes objective, audience, and date so performance can be compared across periods without pulling metadata.
rule.rationale: Ad account naming chaos makes bulk optimization and historical comparison extremely slow.

example.note: Campaign: Sales_Lookalike-US-1%_2026-01. Ad Set: LAL-US-1%_FB-Feed_$50d. Ad: Video-15s_v1_2026-01-20. This encodes enough context to know audience, placement, budget type, creative format, version, and date from the name alone.

list.item: Launch: Campaign → Ad Set → Upload Media → Creative → Ad → Review → ACTIVE
A/B test: 2+ ad sets with one variable different (audience/placement/bid), equal budgets, 7+ days
Retargeting funnel: cold → website visitors (7d) → product viewers (14d) → cart abandoners (7d) → exclude purchasers
